topic.name: Renewable Energy Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting the development and deployment of renewable energy sources, specifically biofuels and solar energy.

Document 1: Establishes a Biofuels Investment Trust Fund
provide assistance to farmers, producers, biorefiners, researchers, universities, and other persons or entities involved in the research, development, and deployment, or production of biofuels
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a program

provide competitive grants to states, Indian tribes, and local governments to provide rebates, loans, or other incentives to eligible participants for the purchase and installation of solar energy systems

topic.name: Environmental Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at the conservation and protection of endangered species and their habitats, as well as water resource management.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to extend deadlines for determining petitions to add or remove species from endangered lists.
Requires conservation measures on Bureau of Land Management land and National Forest System lands.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service to establish a Delaware River Basin restoration program.
Develop criteria to ensure that funded activities restore or protect fish and wildlife and their habitats.

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to health insurance coverage and regulations, particularly focusing on association health plans and Medicaid provisions.

Document 1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to provide for establishment and governance of association health plans (AHPs)
Sets forth rules governing AHPs, including requirements relating to certification, sponsors and boards of trustees, participation and coverage
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make quarterly payments to states with Medicaid plans for 90% of their expenditures for routine HIV screening services
Document 3: Prohibits any of the 12 months beginning with the first month of an applicable individual's loss of health insurance coverage from being taken into account in applying the Internal Revenue Code requirement to maintain minimum essential coverage
Defines 'applicable individual' as an individual who has coverage under an employer-sponsored health plan or individual health insurance coverage for the last month of a plan year

topic.name: Legal Rights and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at defining and protecting individual rights and legal processes within the framework of U.S. law.

Document 1: the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human
each human life begins with fertilization, cloning, or its functional equivalent
Document 2: prohibits the Firearms Trace System database from being immune from legal process
shall be subject to discovery and admissible as evidence
Document 3: authorize any state or local government or related official to file a motion to modify or terminate a federal consent decree
places the burden of proof with respect to such motions on the party originally filing the action

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the welfare and educational opportunities for vulnerable groups such as homeless youth and orphans.

Document 1: Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act's program of grants to states for the education of homeless youths
ensure that homeless youth do not lose credits earned in other schools
Document 2: Establishes within the Department of State the Office for Orphan Policy Diplomacy and Development
supports the preservation and reunification of families and the provision of permanent parental care for orphans
Document 3: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise requirements for public housing agency inspections
Requires the PHA to provide the family a period of time to lease a new residence to assist with the tenant-based rental assistance

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Approvals
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to public works, cultural commemorations, and tribal water rights.

Document 1: Requires the Joint Committee on the Library to accept from the District of Columbia the donation of a statue depicting Frederick Douglass
Requires the Joint Committee to place the statue in a suitable permanent location in Emancipation Hall of the U.S. Capitol.
Document 2: Authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC), upon the request of the licensee for the project numbered 12478-003 (Gibson Dam, Montana), to extend for six years the time period during which the licensee is required to commence construction.
Commences such time period upon expiration of the previous extension issued by FERC before enactment of this Act.
Document 3: This bill amends the White Mountain Apache Tribe Water Rights Quantification Act of 2010 to specify that settlement funds may be used for the planning, design, and construction of the tribe's rural water system.

topic.name: Transportation Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and regulatory standards within transportation systems.

Document 1: economic study on the impact of low-power FM stations
report to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation
Document 2: immunity from civil liability for reporting suspicious activity
passenger transportation security system employees
Document 3: Event Data Recorder Enhancement Act
modification of federal motor vehicle safety standards

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the regulation and oversight of financial institutions to ensure stability and compliance within the financial system.

Document 1: Establishes the Office of the Independent Monitor to determine the compliance with its terms of all parties to the amended consent orders
enforcement actions for unsafe and unsound practices related to residential mortgage loan servicing and foreclosure processing
Document 2: amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision and prudential standards
Document 3: Amends the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 to exempt specified advisers of small business investment companies
exemption with respect to any state or local law requiring the registration, licensing, or qualifications of investment advisers

topic.name: Sustainable Energy and Development
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting sustainable energy solutions and cooperative economic development.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to carry out a research and development program to improve the energy efficiency, reliability, and capacity of wind turbines.
Requires awards under such programs to be made on a competitive basis with an emphasis on technical merit.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish a National Cooperative Development Program to create jobs and increase economic development in underserved areas.
Defines 'cooperative organization' as an autonomous association of persons united voluntarily to meet their common economic, social, and cultural needs.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of General Services to establish an Office of High-Performance Green Buildings.
Requires the Office Director to develop and recommend a high-performance green building research plan.

topic.name: Agricultural Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to agricultural commodities and their marketing regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to include dairy products as a covered commodity.
Requires the retailer of a covered commodity that is a dairy product to designate such commodity's country of origin.
Document 3: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat.
States that in calculating a separate effective price for a class of wheat the effective price shall equal the sum of...

topic.name: Veteran Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to benefits and pensions for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Gold Star Parents Annuity Act of 2007
special pension to each person who has received a Gold Star lapel button
Document 2: Disabled Veterans Commissary and Exchange Store Benefits Act
use commissary and exchange stores on the same basis as a member of the Armed Forces
Document 3: Amends title II of the Social Security Act
eligibility for benefits under the OASDI program

topic.name: Health Policy and Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving health care policies and support systems for various medical conditions and caregiving services.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to repeal the requirement that the Organ Procurement and Transplantation Network adopt and use standards of quality for the acquisition and transportation of donated organs that include standards for preventing the acquisition of organs infected with the etiologic agent for acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS).
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop and publish guidelines for the conduct of research relating to transplantation of organs from HIV-infected donors.
Document 2: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to authorize additional FY2009-FY2011 appropriations for the family caregiver support program.
Authorizes additional FY2009-FY2011 appropriations for the National Clearinghouse for Long-Term Care Information.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Cavernous Angioma Clinical Care, Awareness, Research, and Education Center of Excellence.
Develop the Center as a model and training facility for other facilities throughout the United States that are engaged in research regarding, and care for individuals with, cavernous angioma.

topic.name: Education Policy Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving educational outcomes and accountability for diverse student populations in the U.S. school system.

Document 1: Amends the school improvement program under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
require states to include in their annual state report cards information on military-connected students
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to revise requirements relating to annual yearly progress (AYP)
authorizes the Secretary of Education to modify an AYP timeline
Document 3: Amends part A of title I (Improving the Academic Achievement of the Disadvantaged) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
alters the formula for determining the share of targeted grant funds and education finance incentive grant funds

topic.name: Federal Land Use and Tribal Recognition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation regarding the management and use of federal lands, including the recognition and rights of tribal nations and the facilitation of recreational opportunities.

Document 1: Extends all federal laws and regulations to the Duwamish Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized tribes.
Document 2: Amends the National Forest Ski Area Permit Act of 1986 to require the term and acreage of permits for the operation of ski areas.
Provides for the issuance of permits for the use and occupancy of suitable lands within the National Forest System for skiing and other snow-sports.

topic.name: Small Business Lending and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing lending options and regulatory assistance for small businesses through various programs administered by the Small Business Administration (SBA).

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to collect no fees currently charged by the SBA to borrowers and lenders under the SBA's 7(a) loan program
Increases maximum limits on SBA loans made to small businesses under the Small Business Act
Document 2: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program
Authorizes the Administrator to make one percent, 20-year loans of up to $3 million to up to 20 lending intermediaries.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a program to provide regulatory compliance assistance to small businesses
Requires the Administrator to enter into arrangements with participating Centers to provide access to regulatory information and resources.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Law Enforcement
topic.description: Legislation and measures related to the enforcement of immigration laws and the management of noncitizen individuals in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) and related federal law with respect to immigration law enforcement within the United States.
Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) of the Department of Justice (DOJ) with all information that DHS has regarding any alien.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt certain returning H-2B aliens (temporary nonagricultural worker) from annual numerical limitations.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to ensure that all applicants for law enforcement positions with U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) receive polygraph examinations before being hired.

topic.name: Child Abduction and Exploitation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at addressing child abduction, international child trafficking, and the notification requirements for sex offenders traveling abroad.

Document 1: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act to expand the definition of 'missing child.'
Establishes a grant program to reimburse travel costs related to the return of abducted children to the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Sex Offender Registration and Notification Act to require sex offenders to notify their intent to travel from the United States to another country.
Establishes the Child Exploitation Investigations Center within the CCC to coordinate all agency child exploitation initiatives.

topic.name: Federal Legislation on Infrastructure and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws aimed at managing and improving services and infrastructure related to border security, environmental regulation at ports, and national debt reduction.

Document 1: directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to provide payment to first responders for costs associated with providing emergency services at U.S. international borders
costs are not otherwise reimbursed through any federal program
Document 2: federal preemption of state and local law relating to a price, route, or service of any motor carrier of property shall not apply
adopt requirements for motor carriers providing services at port facilities that are reasonably related to the reduction of environmental pollution
Document 3: Establishes in the Treasury the National Debt Reduction Trust Fund
use such revenues to reduce the national debt

topic.name: Government Record Management and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating the management and accessibility of government records, ensuring compliance and accountability in governmental operations.

Document 1: prohibits the Secretary of a cabinet-level executive department from maintaining a private email server
a Secretary who violates such prohibition is subject to a fine and/or prison term
Document 2: requires the Public Printer to make bills and resolutions available in an electronic format
accessible through the Internet
Document 3: directs the Secretary of State to conduct annual assessments of the effectiveness of sanctions against Iran
in conjunction with the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of Commerce, and the Secretary of Defense

topic.name: U.S. Intelligence and Defense Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to U.S. intelligence operations, military funding, and counterterrorism initiatives.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2014 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2014, for such activities are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations for FY2014.
Document 2: This bill prohibits the President from using Department of Defense (DOD) funds to transfer or release to or within the United States, its territories, or possessions Khalid Sheikh Mohammed
No amounts authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available for DOD for FY2016 may be used to: (1) close or abandon Guantanamo.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to develop, submit to Congress, and implement within one year after enactment of this Act a plan to transfer the headquarters of AFRICOM from Stuttgart, Germany, to a location in the continental United States
Requires the plan to provide for: (1) the 65th Air Base Wing to be an AFRICOM asset.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Military Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses U.S. legislative actions related to trade tariffs and military partnerships.

Document 1: United States-Philippines Security Partnership Enhancement Act of 2015
amends the Arms Export Control Act to extend special foreign military sales status to the Philippines.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain smooth nonwoven fiberglass sheets.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
suspend temporarily the duty on cyan 1 RO inkjet printing ink.

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Passenger Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on improving airline passenger rights, safety measures, and noise mitigation related to aviation operations.

Document 1: Airline Passenger Bill of Rights Act of 2007
establish procedures for handling passenger complaints
Document 2: passenger facility fee imposed at a large hub airport
eligible project cost to carry out noise mitigation
Document 3: Saracini Aviation Safety Act of 2015
installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck

topic.name: Offshore Energy Regulation
topic.description: Legislative measures concerning the regulation and environmental impact of offshore energy exploration and production.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to require detailed environmental analysis of proposed actions
Defines 'offshore drilling activity' as drilling for oil or gas under a lease
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to repeal limitations on export of Outer Continental Shelf oil or gas
Directs the Secretary of the Army to take into account solely domestic environmental impacts when completing an environmental impact statement
Document 3: Allows tax credits for investment in an offshore facility using wind to produce electricity
Defines 'offshore facility' to include the outer Continental Shelf of the United States

topic.name: Political Campaign Finance and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to political contributions, candidate financial disclosures, and student loan interest provisions affecting campaign financing.

Document 1: $200 tax credit for contributions to federal candidates
tax deduction for political contributions up to $600
Document 2: interest payment on loans deferred due to unemployment
supports borrowers in financial distress
Document 3: require candidates to file income tax returns with the FEC
public disclosure of tax returns for presidential candidates

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Depreciation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts related to tax incentives, specifically focusing on depreciation allowances and estate tax provisions.

Document 1: extends additional depreciation allowance for nonresidential real property
applies to Gulf Opportunity Zone
Document 2: makes permanent the repeal of estate and generation skipping transfer taxes
reduces gift tax
Document 3: extends increased expensing allowance for depreciable business assets
provides additional 50% depreciation allowance for investment property
